title: Blackpool: housing regeneration framework and neighbourhood transformation programme
description: Blackpool Council has published a Housing Regeneration Framework and associated programme information setting out how the council plans and coordinates housing-led regeneration and neighbourhood improvements across the borough as part of its wider regeneration activity.
statusRationale: The council has published and updated programme and framework material, indicating ongoing development and delivery planning for housing regeneration activity rather than a single completed capital scheme.
locationDescription: Blackpool borough (representative central point: Blackpool town centre area, Lancashire)
locationSource: Project described as borough-wide across Blackpool in Blackpool Council regeneration pages (Housing Regeneration Framework; Transforming Blackpool's housing)

evidence.title: Housing regeneration framework
evidence.summary: Council page setting out the Housing Regeneration Framework and approach for housing-led regeneration activity in Blackpool.

evidence.title: Transforming Blackpool's housing
evidence.summary: Council programme page describing activity to improve and regenerate housing in Blackpool, including neighbourhood-focused interventions and delivery context.

title: Solihull: Mell Square (Holbeche Place) town centre redevelopment with Muse
description: Solihull Metropolitan Borough Council is progressing the redevelopment of Mell Square as a mixed-use scheme (now referenced as Holbeche Place), with proposals including new homes, retail/leisure uses and new public spaces as part of the wider town centre masterplan.
statusRationale: The council has publicly confirmed partnership and approvals to take the masterplan forward, indicating the project is advancing through development and delivery stages.
locationDescription: Mell Square (Holbeche Place) shopping centre / town centre redevelopment site, Solihull, West Midlands.
locationSource: Solihull Metropolitan Borough Council – Mell Square (Holbeche Place) masterplan and redevelopment announcement.

evidence.title: Solihull Council and Muse sign agreement for the landmark redevelopment of Mell Square
evidence.summary: Council announcement confirming a development agreement with Muse to regenerate Mell Square, describing mixed uses including new public spaces, leisure facilities and homes.

evidence.title: Mell Square Masterplan (latest update: 9 February 2026)
evidence.summary: Council masterplan page providing the latest update on the Mell Square (Holbeche Place) redevelopment, including confirmation of approved plans and next steps.

title: County Durham: Highway Projects Programme of Works 2024–2026 (contract award)
description: Durham County Council has awarded a contract for its Highway Projects Programme of Works 2024–2026, supporting delivery of planned highway improvement and civil engineering works across the county.
statusRationale: A contract award notice has been published for the 2024–2026 highway works programme, indicating delivery arrangements are in place.
locationDescription: County Durham, represented by a central point in Durham city (county town) as the programme covers highway works across the county.
locationSource: Find a Tender award notice 015617-2025 describing a county-wide Highway Projects Programme of Works for Durham County Council.

evidence.title: Highway Projects Programme of Works 2024-2026 (contract award notice)
evidence.summary: Contract award notice for Durham County Council’s Highway Projects Programme of Works 2024–2026.

title: Cheshire East: Middlewich Eastern Bypass main construction phase approved
description: Cheshire East Council has approved the main construction phase for the Middlewich Eastern Bypass, a new road scheme intended to improve connectivity and reduce congestion while supporting planned growth around Middlewich.
statusRationale: The council has publicly approved the main construction phase and set out planned delivery milestones, indicating the scheme is moving from approvals into delivery.
locationDescription: Middlewich Eastern Bypass (representative point east of Middlewich town centre), Middlewich, Cheshire East, England, UK
locationSource: Cheshire East Council news release about the Middlewich Eastern Bypass main construction phase

evidence.title: Green light for construction of Middlewich Eastern Bypass main phase as new link road planned for next year
evidence.summary: Council news release announcing approval of the main construction phase for the Middlewich Eastern Bypass and setting out the planned programme.

title: Huntingdonshire: Priory Centre redevelopment in St Neots (works underway)
description: Huntingdonshire District Council is refurbishing and expanding the Priory Centre in St Neots as part of its Market Towns Programme, aiming to create a modern cultural and community venue supporting town centre vitality.
statusRationale: The council reports that refurbishment and expansion works have officially begun on site, indicating active delivery.
locationDescription: The Priory Centre, St Neots, Cambridgeshire (Huntingdonshire District)
locationSource: Huntingdonshire District Council news release: 'Works Underway on Priory Centre Redevelopment in St Neots' (2025-06-11)

evidence.title: Works Underway on Priory Centre Redevelopment in St Neots
evidence.summary: Council announcement stating that refurbishment and expansion work has started at the Priory Centre, with delivery partners and funding contributions described.

title: West Lindsey: Gainsborough regeneration projects (Whitton Gardens and Baltic Mill) nearing completion
description: West Lindsey District Council reported significant progress on two Gainsborough regeneration schemes: Whitton Gardens (including a new café facility) and the Baltic Mill site transformation into a new green public space, as part of the council’s wider regeneration programme.
statusRationale: The council states both projects are in the final stages of development with completion expected in spring/summer 2025, indicating delivery is well advanced.
locationDescription: Gainsborough town centre, West Lindsey District (representative point for the Whitton Gardens and Baltic Mill regeneration sites in Gainsborough, Lincolnshire).
locationSource: West Lindsey District Council news release describing projects at Whitton Gardens and the Baltic Mill site in Gainsborough; no precise site coordinates provided.

evidence.title: Flagship regeneration projects nearing completion in Gainsborough
evidence.summary: Council update describing progress on Whitton Gardens café works and the Baltic Mill site public space transformation, with anticipated completion timings.

title: Wigan: Galleries town centre redevelopment (market hall construction phase starts)
description: Wigan Council and development partner Cityheart confirmed agreements enabling principal contractor Galliford Try to begin the first construction phases of the former Galleries site redevelopment, including construction of a new 90,000 sqft market hall and enabling works across the wider mixed-use scheme.
statusRationale: The council states construction work is commencing with a principal contractor mobilising on the first phases, indicating active delivery.
locationDescription: Former Wigan Galleries Shopping Centre site, Wigan town centre (The Galleries, Wigan, Greater Manchester)
locationSource: Wigan Council news release referencing works commencing at the former Galleries shopping centre site in Wigan town centre

evidence.title: Construction works to commence at Wigan's former Galleries shopping centre site following agreement signing
evidence.summary: Council announcement that agreements were signed enabling Galliford Try to start the first construction phases, including the new market hall and enabling works across the wider redevelopment.

title: Blaby: Huncote Leisure Centre landfill-gas mitigation minor works (contract award)
description: Blaby District Council awarded a contract for minor works at Huncote Leisure Centre, including installation of gas monitoring wells and works to extend landfill gas mitigation infrastructure to support site safety and potential future reopening.
statusRationale: A transparency notice has been published confirming the decision to award the specialist minor works contract for landfill gas mitigation-related works at the leisure centre site.
locationDescription: Huncote Leisure Centre, Sportsfield Lane, Huncote, Blaby District, Leicestershire
locationSource: Project 'currentLocationDescription' citing Find a Tender notice 050547-2025 (Blaby District Council)

evidence.title: Huncote Leisure Centre Minor Works (transparency notice)
evidence.summary: Transparency notice describing minor works at Huncote Leisure Centre including gas monitoring wells and associated site works tied to landfill gas mitigation infrastructure.

title: Brent: South Kilburn Regeneration Programme (single delivery partner procurement pipeline)
description: The London Borough of Brent published a pipeline notice for procurement of a single delivery partner to support delivery of the final phases of the South Kilburn Regeneration Programme, described as a multi-site regeneration programme with significant housing capacity over a long delivery horizon.
statusRationale: A UK1 pipeline notice has been published for procurement of a delivery partner, indicating the programme is moving through procurement preparation for subsequent delivery.
locationDescription: South Kilburn, London Borough of Brent (representative central point for the South Kilburn regeneration area).
locationSource: Project description and Find a Tender notice location stated as South Kilburn, London Borough of Brent.

evidence.title: Single Delivery Partner - South Kilburn Regeneration Programme (pipeline notice)
evidence.source: Find a Tender (London Borough of Brent)
evidence.summary: Pipeline notice describing Brent’s planned procurement for a single delivery partner to support delivery of later phases of the South Kilburn Regeneration Programme.

title: Salford: Salford Crescent station third platform upgrade (£21m investment)
description: Network Rail is delivering a £21m upgrade at Salford Crescent station to add a third platform and associated works to improve passenger flow and reduce train delays on busy routes through Greater Manchester.
statusRationale: Network Rail has published project updates confirming active construction and delivery of the third platform scheme.
locationDescription: Salford Crescent railway station, Salford, Greater Manchester
locationSource: Project description and Network Rail Media Centre articles referencing works at Salford Crescent station

evidence.title: £21m investment at Salford Crescent station to improve train services across the North West
evidence.summary: Network Rail announcement describing the £21m third platform scheme at Salford Crescent, intended to improve passenger flow and reduce delays.

evidence.title: Salford Crescent third platform: the story so far
evidence.summary: Network Rail progress update describing works completed during early 2025 closures and ongoing construction activities for the third platform.

title: Darlington: £140m station transformation (final footbridge link installed)
description: As part of the ongoing Darlington station transformation programme, Network Rail reported installation of final sections of a new footbridge linking the historic station to the new eastern entrance and new platforms, supporting improved accessibility and station capacity.
statusRationale: Network Rail reports physical milestone delivery (footbridge sections installed) as part of the wider station upgrade programme.
locationDescription: Darlington railway station (Bank Top), Darlington, County Durham
locationSource: Project location explicitly stated as 'Darlington station (Bank Top), Darlington' and corroborated by Network Rail Media Centre article about works at Darlington station.

evidence.title: Final footbridge link installed at Darlington station as £140m transformation progresses
evidence.summary: Network Rail update announcing installation of final sections of the new footbridge connecting the existing station with the new eastern entrance and platforms as part of the £140m redevelopment.

title: North Somerset: Banwell bypass enabling works restarted
description: North Somerset Council restarted environmental enabling works for the Banwell bypass scheme, a new road bypass intended to relieve congestion and support wider transport and development objectives, including associated walking and cycling routes and biodiversity measures.
statusRationale: The council reported enabling works had restarted while the main construction delivery arrangements progressed, indicating preparatory delivery activity rather than completion.
locationDescription: Banwell, North Somerset (representative point for the Banwell bypass scheme area)
locationSource: North Somerset Council news release: 'Environmental works restart on the Banwell bypass' (2024-04-15)

evidence.title: Environmental works restart on the Banwell bypass
evidence.summary: Council announcement that environmental enabling works restarted for the Banwell bypass, describing the nature of preparatory works and the scheme’s aims.

title: Newark and Sherwood: Ollerton town centre regeneration (planning application submitted)
description: Newark and Sherwood District Council progressed the transformation of Ollerton town centre by submitting a formal planning application for a regeneration project intended to revitalise the town centre with new public realm and facilities.
statusRationale: The council reports that a planning application has been submitted, indicating the project is progressing through planning and pre-delivery phases.
locationDescription: Ollerton town centre, Ollerton, Newark and Sherwood District, Nottinghamshire
locationSource: Project description and Newark & Sherwood District Council news release referencing 'Ollerton town centre regeneration' (site not further specified)

evidence.title: Ollerton’s future takes shape as planning permission is submitted
evidence.summary: Council news release announcing submission of a planning application for an Ollerton town centre regeneration project and outlining key proposed elements.

title: Northumberland: Bedlington station opening completes Northumberland Line programme (for now)
description: Northumberland County Council reported the opening of Bedlington station, marking completion of the Northumberland Line programme phase and expanding passenger rail connectivity in south east Northumberland following the line’s reopening.
statusRationale: The council reports a station opening and passenger service commencement milestone, indicating delivered operational infrastructure.
locationDescription: Bedlington station (Northumberland Line), Bedlington, Northumberland, England

evidence.title: Station opening completes Northumberland Line...for now
evidence.summary: Council announcement describing the first passenger train into Bedlington and positioning it as completion of the Northumberland Line programme phase.

title: Eastbourne: Old Orchard Road redevelopment (affordable housing design-and-build tender)
description: Eastbourne Borough Council published a tender notice for the design and build redevelopment of a vacant council-owned site on Old Orchard Road, with plans for 30 affordable flats and landscaped communal amenity space.
statusRationale: A tender notice has been published for the design-and-build redevelopment, indicating the scheme is moving through procurement toward delivery.
locationDescription: Old Orchard Road, Eastbourne town centre, East Sussex (representative point on Old Orchard Road near the town centre as no precise site boundary/address provided).
locationSource: Find a Tender notice 068125-2025 (Eastbourne Borough Council) referencing a vacant council-owned site on Old Orchard Road, Eastbourne.

evidence.title: Old Orchard Road Redevelopment (tender notice)
evidence.summary: Tender notice describing Eastbourne Borough Council’s proposed design-and-build scheme for a vacant Old Orchard Road site with 30 affordable flats and landscaping.

title: Waltham Forest: planned works and responsive repairs delivery partner (pre-procurement engagement)
description: The London Borough of Waltham Forest issued a preliminary market engagement notice for planned works and responsive repairs to its housing stock, to inform its procurement approach for maintenance and repair delivery capacity.
statusRationale: A preliminary market engagement notice has been published, indicating the council is shaping a procurement strategy and scope prior to tender.

locationSource: Project description and Find a Tender notice indicating works to LB Waltham Forest housing stock (no single site specified)

title: Bath and North East Somerset: Bath Walking, Wheeling and Cycling Links scheme consultation
description: Bath & North East Somerset Council launched a public consultation on the Bath Walking, Wheeling and Cycling Links (BWWCL) scheme, proposing improvements across four routes to expand cycling provision and upgrade walking and wheeling infrastructure in Bath.
statusRationale: The council has consulted on route proposals as part of business case and design development, indicating the project is in pre-construction development.
locationDescription: Bath city centre, Bath and North East Somerset (representative point for the Bath Walking, Wheeling and Cycling Links routes across Bath)
locationSource: Bath & North East Somerset Council newsroom releases describing active travel route improvements across Bath

evidence.title: Council launches consultation on active travel routes across Bath
evidence.summary: Council newsroom release launching consultation on the Bath Walking, Wheeling and Cycling Links scheme and outlining the four proposed routes and intended active travel improvements.

evidence.title: Bath active travel consultation extended
evidence.summary: Council update extending the BWWCL consultation period and reiterating the scope of the proposed walking, wheeling and cycling improvements.

title: Harrow: Household Waste and Recycling Centre resurfacing works (contract award)
description: The London Borough of Harrow awarded a one-off contract to resurface the council’s Household Waste and Recycling Centre (HWRC) site at Forward Drive, supporting maintenance and usability of civic waste infrastructure.
statusRationale: A contract details notice has been published showing the resurfacing works contract was awarded and scheduled for delivery.
locationDescription: London Borough of Harrow Household Waste and Recycling Centre (HWRC), Forward Drive, Harrow HA3, Greater London, UK
locationSource: Find a Tender notice 067563-2025 (London Borough of Harrow) referencing the HWRC site at Forward Drive, Harrow HA3
